summary:
**Claim**: Elevated white cell count is the most common laboratory finding in early severe sepsis.  
**Evidence**: - White blood cell (WBC) elevation occurs due to systemic inflammation and immune activation in response to infection. - It is one of the earliest detectable lab changes preceding other markers like lactate rise or thrombocytopenia.  
**Answer**: A  

(498 tokens)
